arts.ttl: Leeds United v Preston: Farke must axe Piroe after Ramazani stance
arts.sum: Leeds United winger Largie Ramazani hasn’t kicked a ball in April, but has become a major talking point because of his lack of action, with Daniel Farke discussed non-negotiables that are keeping him out of the side. What has Daniel Farke said about Leeds United winger Largie Ramazani?

arts.ttl: Scott Parker bemoans fixture schedule as Burnley bid to crank up pressure on Leeds United
arts.sum: On striker Foster, Parker said: “It's not looking too good at the moment, but we're still trying to get a second opinion at this moment in time. Despite having the opportunity to apply some early pressure in the title fight, head coach Scott Parker has bemoaned the fact his side are playing before their promotion rivals this time around.

arts.ttl: Leeds United and Hull City primed for battle to sign 24-year-old Austria Wien player
arts.sum: Leeds United are set to do battle with Hull City for Austria Vienna midfielder Abubakr Barry in the summer transfer window. That’s according to Kronen Zeitung, who name Leeds and Hull as suitors for the 24-year-old midfielder today. They explain that Barry has been impressing since joining the Austrian club on a free transfer from The post Leeds United and Hull City primed for battle to sign 24-year-old Austria Wien player appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Leeds United News: Manager of the Month curse for Sheffield United?
arts.sum: Sheffield United face the long trip down to Home Park to face Plymouth Argyle at the same time as Leeds’ clash with Preston, with Chris Wilder hoping to restore the winning feeling for the Blades. Tyrese Campbell scooped up the player prize while Wilder won the manager award – a deserved reward for four wins and one draw in five games across the month, responding emphatically to Leeds’ 3-1 Bramall Lane win at the end of the previous month.

arts.ttl: Leeds United, Sheffield United, Burnley Championship run-in compared as Whites get key advantage
arts.sum: It's currently advantage Leeds heading into the final five games of the season, not just because of their current standing but the fact that both Burnley and Sheffield United are set to play each other on Easter Monday. Like Leeds, three of their final five games are at home, but face a trip to Burnley which could decide the title race.

arts.ttl: Leeds United sent clear Premier League warning as promotion race heats up
arts.sum: Former Manchester United defender Gary Neville has warned that newly-promoted teams in the Premier League will continue to be punished if they do not adapt one particular element of their style of play. Neville feels that, while the concept of playing out from the back serves teams at the top end of the Championship extremely well, persisting with that style of play in the Premier League tends to be the downfall of newly promoted sides.

arts.ttl: ‘Rampant from the off’ – Leeds United vs Preston North End prediction made by former EFL player
arts.sum: Leeds United have been predicted to win 3-0 this weekend against Preston North End by former EFL player… The post ‘Rampant from the off’ – Leeds United vs Preston North End prediction made by former EFL player appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Leeds United have new secret weapon that Farke could unleash in the run-in
arts.sum: Attaining players who have versatility is something Daniel Farke likes about his Leeds United squad. His cameos earlier this campaign mostly came at left-back, His starts in the cup game at right-back.

arts.ttl: Leeds United transfers: Farke proven right by Sheff Utd flop
arts.sum: Leeds United fans were left incredibly frustrated by the lack of movement in the transfer market regarding a striker signing in January, but others in the Championship are proving that a hasty pursuit can backfire. One player linked with a move to the club during the January window, that was clearly available too, was Sevilla flop Kelechi Iheanacho.
